question:
Say whether each of the boldface numbers is a parameter or a statistic.
(a) Insured? A data set based on 168 hospital discharge summaries shows that $\mathbf{2 0} \%$ of patients were uninsured. (The review takes place in a large referral hospital.)
(b) Percent African American. Data from a standard metropolitan area (SMA) indicate that $\mathbf{1 2}  oldsymbol{\%}$ of the inhabitants are African American. A telephone survey that was based on a random-digitdialing machine found that $8 \%$ of respondents were African American.
(c) Cost of antihypertensive medication. Data from 10 online pharmacies reveal that the average cost of a 1 -month supply of a particular medication is $\mathbf{\$ 3 1 . 2 0}$ with standard deviation $\mathbf{\$ 7 . 7 5}$. Data from 10 community pharmacies shows an average cost of $\$ 33.18$ with standard deviation $\$ 7.88$.
answer:
(a) Statistic
(b) The number $12 \%$ is a parameter. The number $8 \%$ is a statistic.
(c) All are statistics. (These statistics will be used to infer costs at online and community pharmacies, respectively.)
